prompt: Implement a Local Service Commerce & Content Operations Portal for field-service providers that need an offline-first way to publish service offerings, manage customer requests, and govern user-generated content. The Vue.js web app provides role-based entry for Regular Users, Service Agents, Moderators, Administrators, and Data Operators, with English UI and clear, immediate feedback (inline validation, toast confirmations, and status badges). Regular Users can sign in with username and password, maintain a profile with minimal required fields, and view masked contact details by default (for example, (415) ***-1234) while managing favorites, browsing history, and preference settings such as muting specific tags/authors and toggling in-app notifications. Users maintain a US-style address book with create/edit/delete, a single default address, and validation for ZIP code format and required state/city lines; at checkout-like steps for service delivery materials, the UI shows delivery method options (pickup or courier), shipping fees by region/weight/quantity, and an estimated arrival window (for example, "Arrives 3/28/2026, 2:00–6:00 PM") computed from configurable cutoffs. Customers submit service requests with required category, address, preferred time window, and optional attachments (JPG/PNG/PDF up to 5 MB each, max 5 files), then track a ticket lifecycle from Accepted to Dispatched, In Service, Completed, and Closed, with visible SLA timers and the ability to cancel before Dispatch. After completion, users can leave order reviews with 1–5 star ratings, text, and images, see aggregated metrics such as positive review rate, and report abusive content; optional Q&A threads support concise pre-service questions that can be moderated. A unified notification center shows templated messages for status changes, approval reminders, upcoming start/end times, and temporary lockouts, with an outbox view when non-in-app channels are not available. The backend uses Go with Gin to expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend, enforcing RBAC permissions, server-side validation, and consistent error handling. MySQL persists users, sessions, addresses, shipping templates, tickets, ticket notes, review content, moderation actions, and notification templates; sensitive fields are encrypted at rest (AES-256 for phone/address lines) and all transport is secured with TLS even on a local network. Session protection includes HttpOnly cookies, CSRF tokens for state-changing requests, 30-minute inactivity timeout, and account lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed logins in 10 minutes; anti-abuse rate limiting caps API calls at 60 requests per minute per user and throttles review/report submissions to 10 per hour. API request signatures (HMAC with rotating server-managed keys) protect internal clients such as ingestion workers. Content governance applies a local sensitive-term dictionary, blocks prohibited terms, and routes borderline items to Moderators; repeated violations trigger escalating enforcement (24-hour posting freeze, then 7-day freeze). Data deletion supports user-initiated export (downloadable local file) and deletion requests that purge or anonymize personal data after 30 days while retaining audit logs for 7 years. Data ingestion orchestration runs fully offline, pulling from local DB tables, log files, and filesystem drops, tracking incremental offsets or updated_at checkpoints with resumable transfers, row-count and schema checks, and layered lakehouse modeling (bronze/silver/gold) stored on local disk with a MySQL-backed metadata catalog, lineage records, schema evolution rules, and lifecycle policies that archive raw data after 90 days and purge after 18 months unless placed on legal hold.
